channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Haiti wonders what's next as gang violence surges and UN peacekeeping mission flops
item.description: When Kenyan police arrived in Haiti as part of a U.N.-backed mission earlier this year to quell gang violence, hopes were high.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Romanians cast ballots in presidential race that could pit nationalist against leftist in a runoff
item.description: Romanians are casting ballots on Sunday in the first round of a presidential election that could pit a far-right nationalist against the incumbent leftist prime minister in the runoff.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Mcgrath, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taiwan's former president says US should prioritize helping Ukraine over her country
item.description: Former Taiwanese President Tsai Ing-wen suggested that the U.S. should focus on helping Ukraine before it prioritizes Taiwan in her keynote speech during the annual summit of the Halifax International Security Forum, Politico reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Man in India regains consciousness before his cremation on funeral pyre: reports
item.description: A 25-year-old man who was declared dead in India regained consciousness shortly before he was set to be cremated this week, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Philippine vice president publicly threatens to have the president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington Post editorial warns of potential Ukraine abandonment under Trump
item.description: The Washington Post’s editorial board released an op-ed on Saturday detailing the potential threats to Ukraine and the U.S. if President-elect Trump moves to strike a deal with his Russian counterparts. “The reason for this surge in fighting is clear: Mr.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Tens of thousands of Spaniards protest housing crunch and high rents in Barcelona
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Tens of thousands of Spaniards marched in downtown Barcelona on Saturday to protest the skyrocketing cost of renting an apartment in the popular tourist destination.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and HERNÁN MUÑOZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As fast fashion's waste pollutes Africa's environment, designers in Ghana are finding a solution
item.description: ACCRA, Ghana (AP) — In a sprawling secondhand clothing market in Ghana’s capital, early morning shoppers jostle as they search through piles of garments, eager to pluck a bargain or a designer find from the stalls selling used and low-quality apparel imported from the West.
item.dc:creator: FRANCIS KOKUTSE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former New York resident arrested over plan to support ISIS
item.description: An Albanian man with dual citizenship in the U.S. was charged Friday for attempting to provide material support to the Islamic State and distributing instructional material on making explosives, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli-Moldovan rabbi living in UAE is missing. Israeli officials fear he may have been kidnapped
item.description: An Israeli-Moldovan rabbi living in the United Arab Emirates has gone missing, with Israeli authorities raising the suspicion he may have been kidnapped as tensions remain high with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Modi's party heads for victory in Maharashtra state election while opposition wins Jharkhand
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Hindu nationalist party headed for a victory Saturday in state elections in politically significant Maharashtra while the opposition won mineral-rich Jharkhand state.
item.dc:creator: ASHOK SHARMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's top military commander calls out US senator for questioning a woman's role in combat
item.description: The first woman to command Canada’s military is calling out a U.S. senator for questioning the role of women in combat.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippine vice president makes public assassination threat against country's president
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said she has contracted a hired killer to assassinate her country's president in a bizarre public threat made amid worsening political divisions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These Peruvian women left the Amazon, but their homeland still inspires their songs and crafts
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Sadith Silvano’s crafts are born from ancient songs. Brush in hand, eyes on the cloth, the Peruvian woman paints as she sings. And through her voice, her ancestors speak.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly alcohol poisoning casts shadow over the Laotian backpacker town
item.description: VANG VIENG, Laos (AP) — A little town known as a backpacker paradise in northern Laos has come under spotlight for a mass poisoning case that has killed at least six tourists and potentially injured scores of others.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: Lebanese officials say Israeli airstrikes have killed at least 20 people and injured dozens in central Beirut, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon’s capital continue without warning.
item.dc:creator: Abby Sewell And Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NATO and Ukraine to hold emergency talks after Russia's attack with new hypersonic missile
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — NATO and Ukraine will hold emergency talks Tuesday after Russia attacked a central city with an experimental, hypersonic ballistic missile that escalated the nearly 33-month-old war.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and VOLODYMYR YURCHUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Bahamas will refinance part of its debt to protect its famous turquoise waters
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The Bahamas announced Friday that it will refinance $300 million of its external debt to free up more than $120 million for marine conservation projects and climate change mitigation.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani city mourns 42 Shiite Muslims who were ambushed and killed in a gun attack
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Protesters in Pakistan's restive northwest chanted anti-government slogans and tensions flared Friday after funeral prayers were held for 42 Shiite Muslims who were ambushed and killed by gunmen a day earlier in one of the region's deadliest such assaults in recent years.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House members call for classified briefing on Turkey's ties to Hamas
item.description: More than three dozen Democratic and Republican lawmakers are calling on the Biden administration to offer a classified briefing on Turkey’s support for Hamas, raising concerns that the U.S.-designated terrorist group may relocate its headquarters to the NATO-allied country.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: A Norwegian student has been arrested on charges of spying on the US for Russia
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A Norwegian student in his 20s was arrested on suspicion of spying for Russia and Iran while working as a guard at the U.S. Embassy in Oslo, authorities in Norway have said. The man, who has not been identified, was ordered to be held in custody for four weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clinton Defense chief: Netanyahu ‘in very bad company’ with ICC indictment
item.description: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and a former defense minister on war crimes allegations.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, The Hill

item.title: Hungary's Orbán vows to disregard international arrest warrant for Netanyahu
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán on Friday denounced the International Criminal Court’s issuing of an arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, saying he would defy it by inviting him to Hungary.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korean leader says past diplomacy only confirmed US hostility
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said his past negotiations with the United States only confirmed Washington’s “unchangeable” hostility toward his country and described his nuclear buildup as the only way to counter external threats, state media said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s former President Bolsonaro and aides indicted for alleged 2022 coup attempt
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro and 36 others, including aides, were indicted by federal police on Thursday for allegedly attempting a coup to keep him in office in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Archaeologists discover 12,000-year-old pebbles that could provide new insights about the wheel
item.description: Researchers gave a detailed look into perforated stones collected out of excavations in Israel, and came to the conclusion that they could have served as spindle whorls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: After 97 UN aid trucks in Gaza are ‘violently looted’ by armed men, food prices soar
item.description: Food prices are rising in Gaza following the robbery of a U.N. convoy over the weekend, during which, drivers were forced to unload aid at gunpoint, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine fires several US-made longer-range missiles into Russia for the first time
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine fired several American-supplied longer-range missiles into Russia, officials said Tuesday, marking the first time Kyiv used the weapons that way in 1,000 days of war.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC
